word_list.word: a bad custom is like a good cake, better broken than kept
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ bˈæd kˈʌstəm ɪz lˈaɪk ɐ ɡˈʊd kˈeɪk bˈɛɾɚ bɹˈoʊkən ðɐn kˈɛpt

senses.definition: used for implying that it is more beneficial to let go of outdated or harmful practices and customs in favor of positive change and improvement

word_list.word: custom is the plague of wise men and the idol of fools
word_list.pronunciation: kˈʌstəm ɪz ðə plˈeɪɡ ʌv wˈaɪz mˈɛn ænd ðɪ ˈaɪdəl ʌv fˈuːlz

senses.definition: used to warn that blindly following customs and traditions can be detrimental to progress and wisdom, and encourages individuals to question and examine established practices

word_list.word: so many countries, so many customs
word_list.pronunciation: sˌoʊ mˈɛni kˈʌntɹɪz sˌoʊ mˈɛni kˈʌstəmz

senses.definition: used to emphasize that people in different countries have different customs and ways of behaving, and that it is important to recognize and respect these differences

word_list.word: custom is the guide of the ignorant
word_list.pronunciation: kˈʌstəm ɪz ðə ɡˈaɪd ʌvðɪ ˈɪɡnɚɹənt

senses.definition: used to suggest that people who lack knowledge or understanding often rely on established habits and traditions as a default guide for decision making

examples.x: The politician refused to consider new policies or ideas, relying instead on the tried-and-true methods of the past, as custom is the guide of the ignorant.

word_list.word: tradition is a guide and not a jailer
word_list.pronunciation: tɹɐdˈɪʃən ɪz ɐ ɡˈaɪd ænd nˌɑːɾə dʒˈeɪlɚ

senses.definition: used to convey the idea that traditions can be beneficial as a guiding principle but can also become an obstacle to progress or adaptation if they are too rigidly followed

examples.x: The company's management recognized the importance of honoring its traditions while also being open to new ideas and innovations, understanding that tradition is a guide and not a jailer.

word_list.word: it is ill striving against the stream
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ˈɪl stɹˈaɪvɪŋ ɐɡˈɛnst ðə stɹˈiːm

senses.definition: used to imply that it is unwise and futile to act contrary to what is generally accepted or practiced, emphasizing the challenge of going against prevailing norms or opinions

word_list.word: the nail that sticks out, gets hammered down
word_list.pronunciation: ðə nˈeɪl ðæt stˈɪks ˈaʊt ɡˈɛts hˈæmɚd dˈaʊn

senses.definition: used to imply that in some groups or societies, people who are different may be criticized or punished, and there is pressure to conform to established norms
